next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 4

The Ostanian Forum on Economic Realignment was held in a conference room on the third floor of the Foreign Affairs Ministry, and it was exactly as tedious as its name suggested. Anya stood near the back wall with a glass of sparkling water she hadn't touched, wearing her cultural attaché smile, while a deputy minister explained his vision for bilateral tariff reform to an audience that was mostly thinking about lunch.

*...the salmon looked good at the buffet earlier, wonder if there's any left...*

*...if Dreher mentions the import quota one more time I'm going to lose my mind...*

*...call Marta, tell her I'll be late, no, text her, if I call she'll want to talk about the bathroom renovation again...*

The usual hum. Thirty-odd bureaucrats and party functionaries in a room meant for twenty, the radiators working too hard, condensation on the windows turning the grey October sky into a smear. Anya let the thoughts wash through her and tried to pick out operational texture from the noise. Mostly there was none. These forums existed so mid-level officials could be seen attending them, and the thoughts in the room reflected that: career anxiety, social positioning, one woman silently ranking the men in the room by attractiveness with a specificity that made Anya want to take notes.

Then she found Damian.

He was standing near the front, to the left of the podium, in conversation with two men she didn't recognize. Dark suit, the posture he wore in government spaces — squared shoulders, the practiced ease of someone who'd grown up performing comfort in rooms like...
open_hooks_to_carry: Anya files a misleading report to WISE, downgrading Damian's threat profile and minimizing Renewal Front intelligence
She reads Sylvia's complete trust during the debriefing — the specific pain of watching someone believe your lie while you read the trust that makes it possible
The false report is a chapter-level event, not a transition between scenes — dramatize the weight of this institutional betrayal
Cultural exchange event: Damian's partial recognition — something about her face, her laugh
The dual-layer conversation: spoken words vs. Damian's unfiltered thoughts
Comic irony: Anya managing her reactions to his embarrassing/flattering thoughts
Anya pushing the connection past protocol because of both strategic need and genuine interest
prev_tail_excerpt:
...anyone.

And she knew he'd almost remembered her.

Pink hair. A punch during a game. A wet paper towel. A name that started to form and dissolved when someone opened a door.

He'd been carrying that memory for fourteen years. Not prominently — it lived in the place where old, unresolved things settle, surfacing when something triggered it and submerging when the present took over. But it was there. She had mattered to him in some way he'd never examined and never quite released, and she was standing right here on this terrace, with a different name and a different life and the same face he'd looked at without placing.

The distance between what he almost knew and what she completely knew was going to shape everything that came next. She was certain of this the way she was certain of the cold against her skin — as a fact, physical and present and not going anywhere.

She was going to have to go back inside. She was going to have to file a report. She was going to have to sit across from Sylvia and construct a version of tonight that would satisfy WISE — careful, plausible, stripped of telepathy and childhood memory and the ache of hearing someone almost say your name.

She set the warm champagne on the stone railing. The glass made a small sound in the quiet. Her breath was visible. Below, the Ministry gardens were dark shapes — hedgerows, gravel paths, the outline of a fountain that wasn't running. The remaining leaves on the trees moved in a wind she could feel on her arms.

Then she smoothed her dress, uncrossed her arms, and walked back into the warm noise of the party.

setups_to_plant.description: Anya's misleading WISE assessment creates a traceable discrepancy that will be exploited by the mole and contribute to operational failures
Establish Anya's growing guilt about the asymmetry — she negotiates with full access to thoughts while others negotiate blind; she lies while reading the trust that enables the lie

state_deltas_required: Anya must file an assessment to WISE about Damian while managing her conflicting knowledge and loyalties; then she must establish contact
Anya commits institutional betrayal by filing a false report — a traceable lie with unforeseen consequences. Contact with Damian is established. She now operates under two deceptions.
